seo.title: Kanchanaburi Travel Guide: Everything You Need to Know (2026)
seo.metaDescription: Complete Kanchanaburi travel guide for 2026. 3-day itinerary, transport from Bangkok, River Kwai history, Erawan Falls tips, budget breakdown, and local food.

intro: Kanchanaburi deserves at least three days — one for the WWII history that put it on the map, one for the turquoise pools of Erawan Falls, and one for the jungle rivers and floating raft hotels that make it unlike anywhere else in Thailand. Located just 2-3 hours west of Bangkok, this province packs an extraordinary range of experiences into a compact area. This guide covers everything from getting there to what to eat, with practical tips that help you make the most of every day.

itinerary.title: 3 Days in Kanchanaburi: The Complete Itinerary

days.title: WWII History: Bridge, Cemetery & Death Railway
activities: Morning: Start at the Kanchanaburi War Cemetery (Don Rak) on Saeng Chuto Road (free) — take time to read the personal inscriptions on the 6,982 bronze plaques
Mid-morning: Cross to the Thailand-Burma Railway Centre (150 THB) directly opposite — the best museum in town for understanding the full Death Railway story
Lunch: Walk 10 minutes north to the riverside restaurants on Maenam Kwai Road — try pla chon (grilled snakehead fish) at a local spot (80-150 THB)
Afternoon: Walk across the Bridge over the River Kwai (free) — step into a refuge bay when the memorial train passes. Visit the JEATH War Museum nearby (50 THB)
Evening: Night market on Saeng Chuto Road for street food — pad thai (40 THB), moo ping skewers (20 THB each), and fresh fruit shakes (30 THB)

days.title: Erawan Falls: Seven Tiers of Turquoise
activities: Early morning: Depart by 7:30 AM for Erawan National Park (65 km, 1.5 hours by bus or motorbike). Arrive before 9 AM to beat the tour groups

Morning: Enter the park (300 THB) and hike to Tier 7 — allow 1.5 hours uphill. Each tier has unique character: Tier 2 has the best swimming, Tier 5 has a rope swing, Tier 7 has the most powerful cascade
Midday: Swim in the turquoise pools and let the fish nibble your feet at Tiers 1-3 — bring water shoes for the slippery limestone
Afternoon: Hike back down, stopping at tiers you missed. Pick up lunch at the park canteen near the entrance (50-80 THB for pad thai or fried rice)
Evening: Return to town. Dinner at a riverside restaurant with Bridge views — try gaeng som pla (sour curry with freshwater fish, 80-120 THB)

days.title: River Adventures & Floating Raft Experience
activities: Morning: Visit Elephant's World sanctuary (1,800 THB half-day including transport) — feed elephants, prepare their food, and join the mud bath and river bathing
OR Morning: Take the scenic Death Railway train from Kanchanaburi to Nam Tok (100 THB, departs 06:11 or 10:35) — the Tham Krasae viaduct section clinging to the cliff face is breathtaking
Afternoon: Check into a floating raft hotel on the River Kwai Noi — River Kwai Jungle Rafts for the original no-electricity experience, or The FloatHouse for luxury floating villas
Evening: Dinner on the floating platform (usually included in raft hotel rates), followed by stargazing from your private deck on the river — no light pollution, just jungle sounds and flowing water

transport.fromBangkok: Buses depart from Bangkok's Southern Bus Terminal (Sai Tai Mai) every 30 minutes from 5 AM to 10 PM, taking 2.5-3 hours and costing 100-150 THB. Minivans from Victory Monument and Mo Chit run frequently for 120-180 THB (2-2.5 hours, faster but cramped). The scenic train departs Bangkok's Thonburi (Bangkok Noi) station at 07:50 and 13:55 daily, taking 3 hours and costing 100 THB for third class — the journey follows the original Death Railway route and is an attraction in itself.
localTransport: Songthaews: Shared pickup trucks cruising main roads in town — 10-20 THB/person for short hops
Motorbike rental: 200-300 THB/day from Maenam Kwai Road shops. Best option for Erawan Falls and independent exploring
Bicycle rental: 50-100 THB/day — the town is flat and the route to the Bridge is an easy 4 km ride
Public bus to Erawan: Bus 8170 from the bus station, 50 THB, runs approximately hourly (1.5 hours)
Grab: Works in Kanchanaburi town but coverage drops sharply outside the center
Private day tours: 1,500-2,500 THB per person covering Erawan + Bridge + War Cemetery
Longtail boats: Required to reach some floating raft hotels — typically arranged by the hotel (500-1,000 THB or included in room rate)

mustTry: Pla chon (snakehead fish) — local freshwater fish grilled whole over charcoal, served with spicy dipping sauce
Gaeng som pla (sour curry with river fish) — a Kanchanaburi specialty using locally caught freshwater fish
Moo ping (grilled pork skewers) — marinated in garlic and coriander root, found at every night market stall (10-20 THB each)
Pad thai — the classic, available everywhere from 40-60 THB per plate at market stalls
Som tam (papaya salad) — ubiquitous and best paired with sticky rice and grilled chicken
Khao lam (sticky rice in bamboo) — sweet coconut sticky rice steamed inside bamboo tubes, sold roadside near Erawan
Fresh fruit shakes — mango, pineapple, and passion fruit shakes from 30 THB at market stalls
foodAreas: Saeng Chuto Road night market — the main evening food market in town with dozens of stalls and rock-bottom prices
Maenam Kwai Road riverside restaurants — tourist-oriented but some serve genuinely good Thai food with river views
Town center morning market — early risers find jok (rice porridge), pa tong ko (fried dough sticks), and Thai coffee
Erawan park canteen — basic but filling Thai dishes (pad thai, fried rice) at the national park entrance
Floating restaurant dining — several restaurants on the River Kwai near the Bridge serve fresh river fish on platforms over the water
tips: The riverside restaurants closest to the Bridge charge tourist prices — walk 10 minutes south on Maenam Kwai Road for identical food at half the price
Ask for freshwater fish at riverside restaurants — pla chon and pla nin (tilapia) are locally caught and fresher than saltwater options
Most floating raft hotels include meals in their rates — the food is simple Thai fare but surprisingly good given the remote jungle locations
Street food at the night market is safe and excellent — 100-200 THB buys a filling meal with drinks
Carry snacks for Erawan Falls — no food is allowed past Tier 3 to protect the wildlife

etiquette: The War Cemetery and Hellfire Pass are sacred memorial sites — maintain respectful silence and avoid overly casual selfies at the graves
Remove shoes before entering any temple, including Wat Tham Suea and small shrines at cave sites
Cover shoulders and knees when visiting temples — this is strictly observed in Kanchanaburi's more traditional communities
At Elephant's World, follow all staff instructions — these are rescued animals, not entertainment props
Don't litter on jungle trails or at waterfalls — Erawan's pristine pools stay beautiful because visitors respect the environment
Ask permission before photographing local people, especially in rural communities near Erawan and Sai Yok
Stand still during the national anthem played at 8 AM and 6 PM in public areas
Avoid touching monks or handing things directly to them — place offerings on the cloth they extend

budget.budget: 500-900 THB/day (~$14-25 USD): Dorm bed or fan room 200-400 THB, street food meals 40-80 THB each, public bus to Erawan 50 THB, free attractions (Bridge, Cemetery). Kanchanaburi is one of Thailand's most affordable destinations outside Bangkok.
budget.midRange: 1,500-3,500 THB/day (~$42-100 USD): Comfortable hotel 1,000-2,500 THB, mix of street food and restaurant meals 200-500 THB, Erawan entrance 300 THB, Death Railway train 100 THB, motorbike rental 200-300 THB, Elephant's World half-day 1,800 THB.
budget.luxury: 5,000-15,000 THB/day (~$140-420 USD): Floating villa at FloatHouse 5,000-12,000 THB (meals included), private guided tours 3,000-5,000 THB, riverside fine dining 500-1,500 THB, spa treatments 1,000-3,000 THB.
